item.title: Version 0.3.76
item.description:
What's New in v0.3.76

Shared Image File Sync

Shared images from iPhone now sync to your Mac as files (not just metadata). Stored at phones/{device}/shared-images/{date}/. Supports HEIC, JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF — original format preserved, no re-encoding.

Per-Table Sync Error Isolation

A missing table on the Mac (e.g., before migration runs) no longer fails the entire sync batch. Other tables sync normally, and failed tables retry next cycle.

Share Extension Fixes

  • No more full-screen overlay — extension is invisible, dismisses instantly
  • Image sharing works: detects format from file headers, loads all UTI types
  • Swipe-to-delete in Shared Items list (removes DB row + image file)

    • Sparkle Changelog Fix

    Release notes now appear in the macOS update dialog (was showing "Bug fixes and improvements" due to wrong auth token).

    CI: Raw xcodebuild

    Switched from tuist xcodebuild to raw xcodebuild for better DerivedData cache reuse and parallel compilation.

item.title: Version 0.3.77
item.description:
What's New

Browser Extension Testing (87 Playwright tests)

  • Full Chromium test suite: lifecycle, tab/nav events, content script interactions (click, input, scroll, selection, reading sessions), URL policy, dashboard UI, video tracking, queue management, sendToHost fallback, browsing trees, settings persistence, delivery pipeline e2e
  • Safari-mode test suite: loads extension with Safari feature flags in Chromium to verify queue cap (50), disabled APIs, delivery.source = "safariExtension"
  • Runs headless via --headless=new

    • Browser Extension

  • sendToHost abstraction for Safari/Chromium parity — falls back to chrome.storage.local when iOS Safari's service worker is suspended

    • iOS Location Tracking

  • Location state log table — records every GPS decision (motion debounce, GPS start/stop, span open/close, system pause/resume, auth changes) for debugging geo gaps
  • Syncs to Mac for remote analysis via MCP

item.title: Version 0.3.78
item.description:
Fix browsing tree tracking

The browsing_tree_id, parent_tab_id, and tree_depth fields in browser_visits were always NULL due to two bugs:

1. Native host dropped tree fields — The BrowserEvent Codable struct in engram-browser-bridge was missing browsingTreeId, parentTabId, treeDepth, navSequenceIndex, and previousURL. Swift's Codable silently drops undeclared fields during decode/re-encode, so these were never written to JSONL.

2. No tree bootstrap on service worker restart — The in-memory browsingTrees dictionary started empty on every service worker wake. Existing tabs had no tree entries until they happened to navigate.

Changes

  • Add 5 missing fields to BrowserEvent struct in engram-browser-bridge
  • Seed all open tabs via initializeBrowsingTrees() during service worker bootstrap
  • Add round-trip test verifying tree fields survive native messaging serialization
  • Strengthen browsing tree Playwright tests (88/88 pass)

item.title: Version 0.3.79
item.description:
OAuth 2.1 authentication for MCP server

The MCP server now supports GitHub-based OAuth 2.1 authentication, following the MCP spec. The server acts as both Resource Server and Authorization Server facade, proxying identity to GitHub.

How it works

  • Clients discover auth via /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource and /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server
  • Authorization Code flow with PKCE (S256) through GitHub OAuth
  • Bearer tokens validated on every MCP request
  • Tokens persisted in SQLite, survive server restarts

    • Configuration

    Set GitHub Client ID, Client Secret, and Allowed Users in the Mac app's MCP settings panel, or via CLI flags (--github-client-id, --github-client-secret, --github-allowed-users).

    OAuth is opt-in — without credentials configured, the server runs without auth (backward compatible).

    Also included

  • Fix browsing tree fields never reaching the database (v0.3.78)
  • Fix CI caching: warm caches on main branch pushes

item.title: Version 0.3.80
item.description:
OAuth 2.1 authentication for MCP server

The MCP server now supports GitHub-based OAuth 2.1 authentication following the MCP spec. The server acts as both Resource Server and Authorization Server facade, proxying identity to GitHub via Authorization Code + PKCE.

Setup

1. Create a GitHub OAuth App at https://github.com/settings/developers

2. Set callback URL to {publicBaseURL}/callback

3. Configure Client ID, Client Secret, and Allowed Users in the Mac app MCP settings (or via CLI flags)

OAuth is opt-in — without credentials, the server runs without auth.

Also included

  • Fix browsing tree fields never reaching the database (v0.3.78)
  • Fix CI caching: warm caches on main branch pushes

item.title: Version 0.3.81
item.description:
Fix OAuth for MCP clients

  • Implement RFC 7591 Dynamic Client Registration (POST /register)
  • MCP clients (Claude Desktop, etc.) were blocked at the registration step — the server advertised a registration_endpoint but returned 501
  • Clients can now complete the full OAuth 2.1 + PKCE flow to authenticate with the MCP server via GitHub

item.title: Version 0.3.82
item.description:
MCP OAuth fix

  • Implement RFC 7591 Dynamic Client Registration (POST /register) so MCP clients can complete the OAuth flow

    • MCP tool filtering

  • Hide screenshot/OCR/media tools (query_screenshots, get_screenshot, query_ocr_activity, search_screen_text) when screenshots are disabled
  • CLI: add --no-screenshots flag to engram-mcp-server

    • CI

  • Release workflow only triggers on tags, removing redundant cache-warming builds on every push to main

    • iOS sync fixes

  • Stale cursors on fresh pairing: first sync now ignores server-provided cursors, ensuring all records are pushed when pairing a new Mac (fixes "23 pending" instead of ">30k pending")
  • locationStateLogs unbounded growth: synced entries older than 7 days are now pruned after each sync cycle
  • Desktop race condition: newly paired Macs are detected and synced immediately instead of waiting up to 60 seconds for the next cycle
  • New test suite: 11 tests covering sync pending counts, cursor logic, and all three bug fixes with mock HTTP client

item.title: Version 0.3.83
item.description:
MCP server security

  • OAuth is now required for HTTP transport — the server refuses to start without --github-client-id, --github-client-secret, and --public-base-url
  • Remove auto session approval — only OAuth-authenticated clients can create MCP sessions
  • Fix bearer validator being dropped on session reset (previously bypassed OAuth after reconnect)
  • Strip trailing slash from --public-base-url to prevent double-slash metadata URLs

    • Documentation

  • Document the two independent auth mechanisms (OAuth for MCP, bearer token for phone sync) in CLAUDE.md

item.title: Version 0.3.87
item.description:
Fix

  • Add locationStateLogs to server sync accepted tables — the migration created the table but the push handler still rejected it
  • Fix infinite sync push loop when the server doesn't return a cursor for a pushed table

item.title: Version 0.3.88
item.description:
Pedometer-driven GPS control (iOS)

GPS is now off by default and only activates when the pedometer detects movement:

  • Starts in low-power mode (significant location changes only, no GPS drain)
  • Pedometer (CMPedometer) is the primary movement signal — >5 steps/min activates GPS
  • CMMotionActivity only used for automotive/cycling detection
  • Stationary debounce reduced from 120s to 30s, moving debounce from 5s to 3s
  • motionUpdate logging throttled to 1 per 60 seconds (was every callback)
  • New debug fields in locationStateLogs: stepsPerMinute, speed

    • Expected: GPS active time drops from ~24h/day to actual movement periods only. locationStateLogs volume drops ~95%.

    Other fixes

  • Add locationStateLogs table to Mac phone sync schema (v4 migration)
  • Add locationStateLogs to server sync accepted tables
  • Fix infinite sync push loop when server doesn't return cursor for a pushed table

item.title: Version 0.3.89
item.description:
Changes

  • iOS sync: cancellable sync & snapshot-based counters — Sync is now cancellable at every network call boundary. Pending counts are frozen at sync start (snapshot MAX(id) per table) and counted down as batches push, preventing the background refresh from overwriting them. After the batch loop, a re-snapshot detects new data and triggers another pass.
  • iOS: always allow removing a paired Mac — The "Remove Mac" button is no longer disabled during sync. Deleting a desktop cancels its in-flight sync and immediately updates the UI status.
  • Server: accept locationStateLogs in phone sync — The table schema existed (migration v4) but was missing from the server's allowed table list, causing silent rejection and an infinite push loop on the phone.
  • Phone-side cursor fallback — When the server doesn't return a cursor for a pushed table, the phone advances the cursor locally from the last record's ID to prevent infinite loops.
  • Serve /.well-known/openid-configuration — ChatGPT's MCP client uses OpenID Connect Discovery instead of RFC 8414. The server now serves the same OAuth metadata on both endpoints.

item.title: Version 0.3.90
item.description:
Fix MCP server compatibility with ChatGPT

Two issues prevented ChatGPT's MCP client from completing the handshake:

1. Empty POST probe → 401 instead of 400: ChatGPT sends an empty POST to /mcp to discover whether authentication is required. The server now runs the auth validation pipeline first, returning 401 with WWW-Authenticate instead of a generic 400 parse error.

2. Double-initialize grace period: ChatGPT sends two initialize requests within milliseconds. The second one no longer triggers a destructive session rotation if the current session was created less than 5 seconds ago, preventing the first session's SSE stream from being destroyed.

item.title: Version 0.3.91
item.description:
Stateless MCP transport for ChatGPT compatibility

Switches the MCP server from stateful (session-based SSE) to stateless (direct JSON responses). This fixes ChatGPT's MCP client which sends fresh initialize requests on every tool call.

Changes

  • Use StatelessHTTPServerTransport — no session IDs, no SSE, accepts initialize at any time
  • Empty probe POST returns 401 + WWW-Authenticate (was 400)
  • Removed session rotation, restoration, and approval handler (net -85 lines)
  • Includes Phase 1 & 2 of iPhone cloud sync (setup tokens, invite flow, key management)

item.title: Version 0.3.92
item.description:
Device management improvements

  • Devices tab (renamed from "Mobile") now shows all paired devices — iPhones, Macs, watches — with platform-specific icons
  • Admin endpoint: DELETE /sync/v1/devices/{deviceID} for remote device removal (bearer auth required)
  • Mac devices paired via cloud sync now visible alongside iPhones
